_comment: Reference: Alibaba qwen_asr v0.0.6 (author repo, transformers host)
C++: ggml CPU compute, weights stored as BF16 in the accuracy GGUF.
Dump points follow the audio-llm pattern (final encoder block is
enc.mel.in            - input log-mel after frontend normalization
enc.subsample.out     - after 3x Conv2d downsampler + flatten + linear
enc.block.23.out      - final encoder block output (1.7B has 24 layers)
dec.audio_injected    - embedding after audio feature scatter
dec.out_before_head   - after final LM RMSNorm, before lm_head
- enc.mel.in is pre-model and bit-identical to the 0.6B path;
- Post-conv / post-accumulation tensors can grow with model
width. Budgets below start from the 0.6B values scaled up
where the wider hidden / FFN meaningfully accumulates more
BF16 drift, and stay tight where the op-graph is shape-
invariant to model width. Tighten further against observed
